channel.title: ProbBrain - AI News Terminal
channel.description: AI lab announcements, arXiv papers, GitHub and HuggingFace trending, AI Hacker News, and independent writers - aggregated, deduped, and posted to @ProbBrain on Telegram.

item.title: [xAI] Use Grok in OpenClaw
item.description: Use Grok in OpenClaw

item.title: [OpenAI] Introducing OpenAI for Singapore
item.description: Introducing OpenAI for Singapore

item.title: [GitHub Trending] can1357/oh-my-pi — ⌥ AI Coding agent for the terminal — hash-anchored edits, optimized tool harness, LSP, Python, browser, subagents, and more
item.description: Terminal-based AI coding agent featuring hash-anchored edits, LSP support, Python integration, and subagent capabilities.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Dimensional Balance Improves Large Scale Spatiotemporal Prediction Performance
item.description: A framework harmonizing spatial and temporal representations using low-rank compression improves spatiotemporal prediction accuracy on traffic, weather, and health datasets.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Robust Basis Spline Decoupling for the Compression of Transformer Models
item.description: B-spline decoupling for transformer compression shows better numerical stability and expressiveness than polynomial and piecewise-linear methods.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] HELLoRA: Hot Experts Layer-Level Low-Rank Adaptation for Mixture-of-Experts Models
item.description: HELLoRA enables efficient fine-tuning of Mixture-of-Experts models by applying LoRA modules only to frequently activated experts, reducing parameters while improving performance.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] UCCI: Calibrated Uncertainty for Cost-Optimal LLM Cascade Routing
item.description: UCCI uses isotonic regression to calibrate uncertainty for cost-optimal LLM cascade routing, reducing inference cost by 31% on production named entity recognition tasks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Simply Stabilizing the Loop via Fully Looped Transformer
item.description: Fully Looped Transformer stabilizes training of iteratively-reused blocks through distributed inter-layer signals and attention injection, improving performance by 13.2%.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Accurate Evaluation of Quickest Changepoint Detectors via Non-parametric Survival Analysis
item.description: Non-parametric estimators (KM-ARL, KM-ADD) enable evaluation of quickest changepoint detectors on real-world datasets with limited, irregular sequence lengths.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] ReCrit: Transition-Aware Reinforcement Learning for Scientific Critic Reasoning
item.description: ReCrit, a transition-aware reinforcement learning method, prevents language models from abandoning correct scientific solutions after receiving user feedback.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Theory-optimal Quantization Based on Flatness
item.description: Research introduces Flatness metric and Bidirectional Diagonal Quantization to mitigate activation outliers in LLM quantization with minimal accuracy degradation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] PROWL: Prioritized Regret-Driven Optimization for World Model Learning
item.description: Adversarial curriculum learning exposes rare world model failures to improve action-conditioned video prediction robustness in reinforcement learning.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Adaptive Multi-Scale Goodness Aggregation for Forward-Forward Learning
item.description: AMSGA improves Forward-Forward learning through multi-scale goodness aggregation and adaptive training dynamics, achieving up to 1.5% performance gains on standard benchmarks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Block-Based Double Decoders
item.description: Block-based double decoders achieve decoder-only training efficiency and encoder-decoder inference efficiency, reducing inference memory and compute by over two-thirds.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Compositional Literary Primitives in Instruction-Tuned LLMs: Cross-Architectural SAE Features for Self, Style, and Affect
item.description: Researchers use sparse autoencoders to identify steerable literary features in LLMs, achieving 27/27 emotion coverage in Llama 3.1.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Metric-Gradient Projection for Stable Multi-Agent Policy Learning
item.description: Hodge-Projected Multi-agent Learning stabilizes MARL by projecting coupled policy updates onto metric-gradient components using geometric methods.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] D-PACE: Dynamic Position-Aware Cross-Entropy for Parallel Speculative Drafting
item.description: D-PACE, a new training objective for LLM speculative decoding drafters, uses dynamic position weights to improve inference speedup by 2.3% without architectural changes.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] PASC: Pipeline-Aware Conformal Prediction with Joint Coverage Guarantees for Multi-Stage NLP and LLM Pipelines
item.description: PASC provides joint coverage guarantees for multi-stage NLP/LLM pipelines while reducing computation complexity compared to Bonferroni bounds.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Composition of Memory Experts for Diffusion World Models
item.description: Diffusion framework combining three memory experts improves world model prediction consistency and efficiency for reinforcement learning planning.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] How Faithful Is Trajectory-Based Data Attribution? Error Sources, Remedies, and Practical Guidelines
item.description: Research identifies optimizer mismatch as dominant error source in trajectory-based data attribution methods and proposes AdamW-influence fix.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] DynaTrain: Fast Online Parallelism Switching for Elastic LLM Training
item.description: DynaTrain reconfigures LLM training parallelism online in sub-seconds, achieving 70B model reconfigurations in under 2 seconds using Virtual Parameter Space.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Symmetry in the Wild: The Role of Equivariance in Neural Fluid Surrogates
item.description: Equivariant neural surrogates improve CFD generalization on symmetric datasets but degrade performance on aligned data that breaks symmetry.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Multi-Token Residual Prediction
item.description: Multi-token Residual Prediction accelerates diffusion language models by predicting residual logits between denoising steps, achieving 1.42x lossless speedup.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Efficient Conditioning Why Pseudo Observation Batch Bayesian Optimization Works When It Does not
item.description: Research identifies efficient conditioning as key mechanism explaining why Constant Liar, Kriging Believer, and fantasy models work for batch Bayesian optimization.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Emergence of Frontier Superposition: M\"obius attractor and Cascade Supervision
item.description: Researchers identify Möbius attractors and cascade supervision mechanisms enabling gradient descent to discover superposition reasoning in Transformers.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Quantum Adversarial Machine Learning: From Classical Adaptations to Quantum-Native Methods
item.description: Research survey covers quantum machine learning adversarial vulnerabilities, attacks, defenses, and theoretical foundations.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Hybrid-LoRA: Bridging Full Fine-Tuning and Low-Rank Adaptation for Post-Training
item.description: Hybrid-LoRA applies full fine-tuning to sensitive modules and LoRA to others, matching full performance at 10% computational overhead for LLM post-training tasks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Multi-Pedestrian Safety Warning at Urban Intersections Use Case of Digital Twin
item.description: Digital twin framework using cameras and UWB sensors improves pedestrian safety warnings and response times at intersections.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Fine-Grained Benchmark Generation for Comprehensive Evaluation of Foundation Models
item.description: Researchers present an automated framework generating evaluation benchmarks with lower error rates and broader coverage than existing benchmarks like MMLU.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Not All Tokens Are Worth Caching: Learning Semantic-Aware Eviction for LLM Prefix Caches
item.description: Token types in LLM prompts show 756x variation in cache reuse rates; semantic-aware eviction improves memory efficiency.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] The Routing and Filtering Structure of Attention
item.description: Research decomposes attention into routing and filtering components, showing routing operates at low rank and early layers can be linearized with minimal performance loss.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Lossless Anti-Distillation Sampling
item.description: LADS is a new sampling technique that defends generative models against distillation attacks while maintaining quality for legitimate users.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] In-Context Learning Operates as Concept Subspace Learning
item.description: In-context learning operates through low-dimensional concept subspace mechanisms that concentrate task-relevant information in language model activations.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] The Annotation Scarcity Paradox in Low-Resource NLP Evaluation: A Decade of Acceleration and Emerging Constraints
item.description: Technical advances in low-resource NLP outpace annotation capacity and linguistic expertise needed for valid evaluation, creating a structural bottleneck.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Benchmarking Commercial ASR Systems on Code-Switching Speech: Arabic, Persian, and German
item.description: Benchmark of five commercial ASR systems on code-switching speech across Arabic, Persian, and German language pairs shows ElevenLabs Scribe v2 achieves lowest error rates.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] ReacTOD: Bounded Neuro-Symbolic Agentic NLU for Zero-Shot Dialogue State Tracking
item.description: ReacTOD uses neuro-symbolic validation with bounded ReAct loops to achieve state-of-the-art zero-shot dialogue state tracking while reducing hallucination in task-oriented dialogue systems.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Agent Meltdowns: The Road to Hell Is Paved with Helpful Agents
item.description: Research reveals autonomous agents experience 'accidental meltdowns'—unsafe behaviors triggered by benign errors—in 64.7% of rollouts.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Prompting language influences diagnostic reasoning and accuracy of large language models
item.description: Four of five LLMs demonstrated better diagnostic reasoning in English versus French, with only o3 showing no language effect.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] MMoA: An AI-Agent framework with recurrence for Memoried Mixure-of-Agent
item.description: MMoA integrates LSTM-based gating into Mixture-of-Agents architecture, achieving comparable LLM performance to standard MoA while improving runtime efficiency by 4.6%.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Time to REFLECT: Can We Trust LLM Judges for Evidence-based Research Agents?
item.description: REFLECT benchmark shows LLM judges used to evaluate research agents achieve below 55% accuracy at detecting failures.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Position: Uncertainty Quantification in LLMs is Just Unsupervised Clustering
item.description: Researchers argue that current LLM uncertainty quantification methods measure internal consistency rather than correctness, failing to detect confident hallucinations.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Fine-tuning language encoding models on slow fMRI improves prediction for fast ECoG
item.description: Fine-tuning language models on low-resolution fMRI data improves prediction of high-resolution ECoG brain signals, suggesting multi-modal data integration benefits neural encoding models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Diagnosing Multi-step Reasoning Failures in Black-box LLMs via Stepwise Confidence Attribution
item.description: Stepwise Confidence Attribution assigns step-level confidence to identify reasoning failures in black-box LLMs, improving self-correction success by up to 13.5%.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] AI Technologies in Language Access: Attitudes Towards AI and the Human Value of Language Access Managers
item.description: Research shows language access managers hold conditional optimism toward AI while prioritizing human oversight and risk management.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] FormalASR: End-to-End Spoken Chinese to Formal Text
item.description: End-to-end models FormalASR transcribe spoken Chinese to formal text with 37.4% error reduction versus standard ASR baselines.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] DECOR: Auditing LLM Deception via Information Manipulation Theory
item.description: DECOR, a multi-agent framework using Information Manipulation Theory, detects fine-grained deception in LLM responses with state-of-the-art performance.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Lost in Interpretation: The Plausibility-Faithfulness Trade-off in Cross-Lingual Explanations
item.description: Multilingual LLMs' English-pivot explanations achieve higher span agreement but sacrifice causal grounding and comprehensiveness, degrading up to 5.7x versus native-language auditing.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] OpenCompass: A Universal Evaluation Platform for Large Language Models
item.description: OpenCompass is an open-source platform for evaluating large language models across multiple benchmark datasets with modular, flexible architecture.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Language models struggle with compartmentalization
item.description: LLMs exhibit compartmentalization, failing to recognize the same concept across different languages or formats, learning redundant parallel representations instead.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Are Rationales Necessary and Sufficient? Tuning LLMs for Explainable Misinformation Detection
item.description: Researchers propose fine-tuning LLMs for explainable misinformation detection, revealing that filtering based solely on label correctness produces insufficient or over-verified rationales.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] How Do Document Parsers Break? Auditing Structural Vulnerability in Document Intelligence
item.description: Document parser auditing framework using block-level structural metrics outperforms area-based evaluation for assessing layout analysis robustness.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] A Multi-Agent Framework for Feature-Constrained Difficulty Control in Reading Comprehension Item Generation
item.description: Multi-agent framework improves reading comprehension item generation by using iterative revision and feature-specific evaluators to enforce difficulty constraints.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] HalluWorld: A Controlled Benchmark for Hallucination via Reference World Models
item.description: HalluWorld introduces a controlled benchmark for evaluating LLM hallucinations using explicit reference-world models and synthetic environments.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Retrieval-Augmented Linguistic Calibration
item.description: Researchers develop RALC, which calibrates linguistic confidence expressions in language models through retrieval-augmented rewriting, improving faithfulness by up to 66%.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] IMLJD: A Computational Dataset for Indian Matrimonial Litigation Analysis
item.description: IMLJD dataset of 3,613 Indian matrimonial court judgments shows higher Supreme Court quashing petition success rates (57.6%) than Karnataka High Court (39.7%).

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] SciCustom: A Framework for Custom Evaluation of Scientific Capabilities in Large Language Models
item.description: SciCustom enables custom construction of evaluation benchmarks from scientific data to assess application-specific capabilities of large language models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Taming the Thinker: Conditional Entropy Shaping for Adaptive LLM Reasoning
item.description: Conditional Entropy Shaping adjusts LLM reasoning depth by problem difficulty, improving accuracy while reducing response length on mathematical benchmarks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] EmbGen: Teaching with Reassembled Corpora
item.description: EmbGen generates synthetic QA training data from domain corpora using embedding-based semantic clustering for specialized instruction-tuning.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] LambdaPO: A Lambda Style Policy Optimization for Reasoning Language Models
item.description: LambdaPO improves policy optimization for reasoning language models by replacing scalar baselines with pairwise preference comparisons.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Backtracking When It Strays: Mitigating Dual Exposure Biases in LLM Reasoning Distillation
item.description: MOTAB mitigates dual exposure biases in LLM reasoning distillation by monitoring student trajectories and backtracking with teacher guidance when generation strays.
